issues.title: Land board M5 auto-start + open-PR-on-done + editable quick-action templates (unmerged feature branches)
issues.body: From the 2026-06-13 follow-up survey. origin/feat/web-kanban-board / origin/feat/conflict-radar carry board commits never merged to main: af5ca31 (board M5 rule-based auto-start + agent self-report turn-complete), de93d68 (open-PR-on-done button + editable quick-action template editor), 048876d (hover-to-move status tags). Real, tested board features extending the active board workstream. Needs reconciliation against main's post-merge chips.tsx/Board.tsx/store.ts (dedupe-React + conflict-badge waves) + changesets. DECISION PENDING (Jackson): landing others'/parallel feature branches is a product/integration call, not done silently.

issues.title: Reconcile and land feat/web-issues-panel (daemon-backed issues board + one-click quick-start)
issues.body: From the 2026-06-13 survey. origin/feat/web-issues-panel is ~20 commits ahead but branched from a 0.7.25-era base before the conflict-radar/attention waves (12 behind main). Feature-complete daemon-owned issue tracker (issues-store.ts, /api/issues, IssuesPage.tsx). Needs: merge onto main, resolve store.ts (channel subs) + chips.tsx + __root.tsx (route tree) conflicts, add the new issue verbs to SPA_CHANNELS contract test + rpc-allowlist, full kobe-web gate, changeset. DECISION PENDING (Jackson): parallel-branch landing.

issues.title: Daemon reconnect + event seq/replay (KOB-38) — recover an attached TUI after `kobe daemon restart`
issues.body: From the survey + docs/design/daemon-d4-followups.md. KobeDaemonClient (packages/kobe-daemon/src/client/index.ts) has NO reconnect logic — a socket close just rejects pending requests, so a daemon restart leaves an attached TUI dead forever, contradicting daemon.md §8's auto-reconnect promise. Biggest daemon correctness gap. Scope: add an event `seq` on the broadcast path in daemon/server.ts + a replay-since-seq subscribe arg; reconnect loop with backoff in client/index.ts re-subscribing with last-seen seq; integration test that restarts the daemon mid-attach and asserts no missed events. Unblocks KOB-39/40. Effort L.

issues.title: Web Settings → Engines: add/remove/rename custom engines (parity with TUI v0.7.26)
issues.body: From the survey. EnginesCard (AppShell.tsx ~696) is READ-ONLY — lists detected vendors, no add/remove/rename — while the TUI shipped user-addable custom engines (VendorId open type, availableEngineIds()) in 0.7.26. Scope: extend EnginesCard with add/remove/rename backed by daemon customEngineIds / engineName.<id> / engineCommand.<id> state (new RPC verbs in server/rpc-allowlist.ts), mirroring the TUI 3-step add flow; surface composed list in the New Task vendor picker; lib/engines.ts compose/validate helper + unit test. Effort M (touches daemon + bridge + web).

issues.title: Structured daemon logging to <KOBE_HOME>/.kobe/logs/ (KOB-40)
issues.body: From the survey + daemon-d4-followups.md item 5. Auto-started daemons spawn with stdio:ignore, so every console.log/warn/error (orchestrator/core.ts, index/store.ts, lockfile.ts) is lost — a diagnostics blackhole on crash. Scope: a structured logger writing to <KOBE_HOME>/.kobe/logs/ with [subsystem] tags + timestamps + levels (follow crash-log.ts precedent), route console.* through it, keep the daemon.log redirect. Prereq for clean observability on KOB-38/39. Effort M.

issues.title: Daemon socket chmod 0600 (KOB-41)
issues.body: From the survey + daemon-d4-followups.md item 3. The code never chmods the unix socket, so default umask gives ~0755 — the documented single-user 0600 auth contract (daemon.md §10) is unenforced. Scope: in daemon/server.ts, after listen, fs.chmod the socket to 0o600; assert the mode in a test. Full token-auth stays deferred (single-user threat model); this only closes the doc-vs-code gap. Effort S, no deps.

issues.title: Per-client backpressure + drain handling in daemon broadcast (KOB-39)
issues.body: From the survey + daemon-d4-followups.md item 2. server.ts broadcast() ignores socket.write()'s return and never handles 'drain', so a frozen/suspended/paused client pins daemon heap unbounded — a real flake risk with multi-attach + 10+ token-streaming tabs. Scope: track per-client write backpressure, buffer/coalesce or drop non-critical chat.delta on a slow consumer, resume on 'drain'; rely on KOB-38 seq/replay to cover dropped deltas; test a paused socket asserts bounded buffering. Depends on KOB-38. Effort L.

issues.title: Glob/negation patterns in the Changes-pane file filter
issues.body: From the survey. diff-filter.ts matchesPath() is a plain substring match (tested in diff-filter.test.ts), so users can't narrow to *.test.ts or exclude !*.json. Scope: extend matchesPath() to detect glob (*.test.ts), negation (!*.json), and anchored (src/) patterns, falling back to substring when no glob chars; add cases to diff-filter.test.ts + a one-line hint in the ChangesList filter input. Small, fully unit-testable, daily-use pane. Effort S.

issues.title: TUI Notes pane + Checks tab (close web-only / placeholder asymmetries)
issues.body: From the survey. Two TUI gaps where the web/spec is ahead: (a) the web NotesPanel has full Edit/Preview markdown but the TUI has NO notes affordance — add a right-rail Notes pane reading/writing the same server-persisted per-task notes the bridge exposes (/api/notes). (b) FileTree.tsx:27 still documents the Checks tab as 'placeholder — CI/test integration not yet implemented'; the third tab renders empty — implement it to shell out to the repo's test+typecheck commands (async), render pass/fail rows. Effort M each, low priority.

issues.title: Web rail filter/status-chip/sort state resets on the first / → /task/$taskId navigation
issues.body: Pre-existing structural issue surfaced by the 2026-06-11 multi-agent review of the morning web waves. The `/` route renders <AppShell/> directly while /task/$taskId renders <TaskRoute> which wraps <AppShell/> one level deeper (routes/index.tsx vs routes/task.$taskId.tsx). At the Outlet these are different component types, so opening a task from the home route unmounts+remounts AppShell, resetting TaskRail's local useState — including the new statusFilter chip, the text query, and showArchived (sortMode is partially re-applied by the prefSort effect). Task→task hops stay within /task/$taskId so they persist; only the initial open from `/` clears it. RESOLVED 2026-06-11: lifted query/statusFilter/sortMode/showArchived into lib/rail-state.ts, a module store (tabs.ts pattern). Persistence semantics decided as in-memory ONLY — survives route nav (the bug), deliberately resets on full reload (a stale text query across visits would surprise; sortMode needs no storage since the TUI ui-prefs push re-seeds it). Bonus: applyPrefSort is rising-edge (tracks last applied pref), so an AppShell remount replaying the same TUI pref no longer stomps a local web-side sort toggle — only a genuinely changed pref does. 6 unit tests lock the contract; QA'd live (1/4 filtered count survives the / → /task hop).

issues.title: Job-ify ensureWorktree: long daemon operations return a job id + push progress
issues.body: Follow-up to the async-ExecHost fix (commit 'fix: exec — async ExecHost run/fs ops'). The daemon's event loop no longer freezes during git worktree add, but the CALLING client still awaits the full RPC (minutes on a 30GB repo) with no feedback. Make minute-class operations (ensureWorktree, future clone) jobs: RPC returns immediately with a job id, progress/completion pushed over the existing subscribe channel, UI shows a materializing state on the task row. Job table + existing push channel — no external MQ; that was the 'daemon 要不要 MQ' answer (2026-06-09 audit).

issues.title: Sink worktree-changes collection into the daemon (single collector, push to panes)
issues.body: Today every pane process polls git status itself through tui/lib/background-poll.ts (N panes x M tasks background processes, work duplicated per pane). Move collection into the daemon as the single collector and broadcast results over a new subscribe channel (worktree.changes), following the task.snapshot precedent; panes drop their git subprocesses entirely and just read pushes. Depends on nothing; pairs well with the job-ification issue. From the 2026-06-09 render-path/daemon audit.

issues.title: Per-client tmux sessions so two clients on the same window aren't letterboxed
issues.body: aggressive-resize (shipped) makes each chat-tab window track its own current viewer, but two clients viewing the SAME task+window still share one tmux grid, so the larger terminal is letterboxed to the smaller. The only way to give same-window clients independent sizes is to stop sharing one session per task: give each attached client its own session in a session group (new-session -t <group>) and rework the switch-client task-switch model (direct.ts, tasks-pane/host.tsx, app.tsx, client.ts) to switch within the client's own session set. Larger refactor; ceiling is capped by tmux (grouped sessions still share window grids), so revisit only if same-window multi-client is a real use case. Deferred from the aggressive-resize fix; user chose the small A-tier fix first.

issues.title: Hover tooltip can flicker when the cursor moves onto it
issues.body: The v1 sidebar hover tooltip (cf1cd56) anchors just below-right of the cursor. Moving the cursor down into the tooltip's area leaves the hovered row → onMouseOut clears it → the row beneath re-pops it, so chasing it flickers. In a full-width tmux Tasks pane there's no room to the right, so below is the only anchor. Option to evaluate: a fixed detail panel pinned to the bottom of the sidebar (no cursor follow, zero flicker) instead of a floating tooltip.

issues.title: Hover tooltip is always-on — consider showing only when info was truncated
issues.body: The tooltip pops on every row hover, including on a wide pane where the branch + full title are already visible (only the worktree path is extra). Consider gating it to fire only when the responsive columns actually dropped the branch/changes or the title was ellipsised, so a wide pane stays quiet.

issues.title: Optional: CI check enforcing a changeset per PR
issues.body: Now that Changesets is adopted, add a CI step (changeset status gate, or the changesets/action bot) that fails a PR with user-facing code changes but no .changeset/*.md, so entries can't be forgotten. Tooling-only changes opt out with an empty changeset. Deferred from the Changesets migration to keep that change focused.
